title: Chevron CEO Wirth on Oil Prices, Strait of Hormuz, Venezuela

He speaks on “Bloomberg Surveillance.” (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Ships Attacked in Strait of Hormuz This Week, Chevron CEO Says
summary: Chevron CEO Mike Wirth discusses the risks for tankers transiting the Strait of Hormuz and says that multiple vessels have been attacked in recent days. “We see risks very real still in that environment,” Wirth said on “Bloomberg Surveillance.” (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Oil Set for May Slide as Traders Bet on US-Iran Truce Renewal
summary: Oil dropped to a six-week low as the US and Iran tentatively agreed to extend a ceasefire by 60 days, stoking optimism the Strait of Hormuz may soon reopen.

title: Zambia Bonds Jump After 2053 Debt Buyback Tender Starts
summary: Zambia launched a cash tender offer to buy back $1.36 billion bond due in 2053, becoming the latest African nation to repurchase outstanding debt as governments seek to reduce their servicing costs.

title: July, August Critical Months for Oil Inventories, Chevron CEO Says
summary: Chevron CEO Mike Wirth discusses the impact of the war in Iran on oil prices and global supplies and says the company would not consider paying a toll to traverse the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: ‘Two Sigma’ S&P Rally Signals Easy Money Has Already Been Made
summary: The S&P 500 Index has returned to all-time highs, yet there’s no mistaking that Wall Street’s breathtaking two-month rally has slowed dramatically.

title: India’s Maritime Financier Readies Nation’s First Blue Bond
summary: Sagarmala Finance Corp., a financier created to fund India’s maritime projects, is preparing to issue the country’s maiden blue bond to raise as much as 10 billion rupees ($105 million), according to a top official.

title: Unknown MAGA Warrior Upends Race for a Seat on Texas Oil Regulator
summary: Hard-right candidate with little connection to the oil industry is primed to win a place on the Railroad Commission of Texas.

title: Inflation Above ECB Comfort Zone in Top Economies Backs Hike
summary: Inflation stayed well above 2% in the euro zone’s four top economies, backing the case for the European Central Bank to raise interest rates for the first time since 2023.

title: IndiGo Posts Shock Loss Amid Forex Drag, Mideast Turmoil
summary: IndiGo, Asia’s largest low-cost carrier, reported an unexpected quarterly loss, amid cost pressures, operational disruptions and softer demand linked to the Middle East conflict.

title: US Premarket Movers for May 29, 2026
summary: S&P 500 Index futures rise 0.1% as of 7:31 a.m. in New York as traders wait to see whether the US and Iran can finally get a peace deal across the line.

title: Japan Used Record $73.6 Billion to Support Yen in Past Month
summary: Japan used a record of almost $74 billion over the past month to prop up the yen after the currency slid past 160 per dollar, according to Finance Ministry data confirming the government’s first market intervention since 2024.

title: Hershey Bull Sees World Cup, America 250 Lifting Chocolate Sales
summary: Summer events including the FIFA World Cup and America’s 250th anniversary could spur sales of sweet treats, making now a key time to snap up beaten-down shares of Hershey Co., according to the candy and snack maker’s newest Wall Street bull.

title: Czech Billionaire Sees Potential to Boost TotalEnergies Stake
summary: Czech billionaire Daniel Kretinsky said he’s open to increasing his holding in TotalEnergies SE, after gaining a stake in the French oil major last month.

title: Fed’s Schmid Says Officials Must Stay Focused on Inflation
summary: Federal Reserve Bank of Kansas City President Jeff Schmid said he remained focused on elevated inflation and officials should make it clear they’re willing to do what is needed to achieve price stability.

title: Gold Rises Above $4,500 as Iran Deal Hopes Ease Inflation Fears
summary: Gold rose as reports that the US and Iran reached a preliminary deal to extend their ceasefire eased expectations of higher-for-longer interest rates.

title: Stocks Hold at Record Highs on US-Iran Deal Optimism | Bloomberg Brief 5/29/2026
summary: Stocks hold steady at all-time highs as traders wait to see whether the US and Iran can finalize a peace deal. SpaceX is said to cut its IPO valuation to at least $1.8 trillion while Anthropic's newest funding round puts its valuation at $965 billion eclipsing rival OpenAI. Cole Smead of Smead Capital Management discusses the 'mania' in tech stocks. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Intesa Seals SRTs on $4.8 Billion in US Corporate and ESG Loans
summary: Intesa Sanpaolo SpA has wrapped up two significant risk transfers tied to corporate loans worth about $4.8 billion as it takes advantage of robust demand to make greater use of the hedging tool.

title: Fed Must Signal Commitment to Inflation, Schmid Says
summary: Federal Reserve Bank of Kansas City President Jeff Schmid said officials should make it clear they’re willing to do what is needed to achieve price stability. “We must continue to signal our commitment to price stability and our willingness to take the actions necessary to achieve our mandate,” Schmid said Friday at a conference in Reykjavík, Iceland. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Aluminum Squeeze Deepens as Spreads Tighten and Inventories Drop
summary: The aluminum market is facing a deepening shortage as the closure of the Strait of Hormuz throttles supply, with spot prices for the metal spiking while exchange inventories slump.

title: Zurich Bank Looks to Brazil, Florida as It Expands in Americas
summary: EFG International AG, a private-banking boutique based in Zurich, is expanding its 300-person team serving clients from the Americas with an emphasis on Brazil and South Florida.

title: Indonesia and India Intervene to Prop Up Weakening Currencies
summary: Indonesia and India intervened on Friday to prop up their currencies, which are being pressured by an energy price spike.

title: Dollar’s Monthly Rise Leaves Strategists Wary of More Gains
summary: This month’s rally in the dollar, as traders priced in the prospect of higher US interest rates, is leaving Wall Street strategists wary of further gains.

title: Tentative US-Iran Deal & SpaceX Cuts Valuation | The Pulse 5/29/2026
summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests: Seema Shah, Principal Asset Management, Chief Global Strategist; Aditi Venkatram, Jefferies, Equity Capital Markets Managing Director; Nate Soares, Machine Intelligence Research Institute, President & Author, ‘If Anyone Builds It, Everyone Dies’ (Source: Bloomberg)
